>
According to the article, Wall Street executives believe "it would be a suboptimal situation for the banks" if Elizabeth were to win the Democratic nomination.

It could be because Elizabeth has taken on the big banks and Wall Street CEOs before, and won.

Maybe they're scared of Elizabeth's plan to stop Wall Street from looting our economy. Or her proposal for an Ultra-Millionaire Tax, which would pay for Universal Child Care and early education, canceling student loan debt, universal free two-year and four-year public college and technical school, and then some.

Or maybe it's because Elizabeth's plans will put American workers ahead of corporate profits and bonuses.

Whatever has them nervous, this headline says that we're doing everything right. They want our movement to stop. But it's only growing stronger.

Elizabeth is running to be the best president that their money can't buy. And her ideas for big, structural change have Wall Street executives shaking in their freshly polished leather shoes. They know we can win. But we can't do this without you.

[**Will you chip in $3 for the first time to support Elizabeth's plans to end Wall Street's stranglehold on our economy and show that this movement will not stop?**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28529dd2/45c30650/133e1bc2a/66884f48/1878770917/VEsHAg/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LTIwMTlxM2ZyIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTUozMkZfUkVTX0hETF9QXzIwMTkwOTEwX1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-2019q3fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMJ32F_RES_HDL_P_20190910_V1S1_P")

Thanks for all you do,

Team Warren

name: Stephen Stepanek
subject: defending our small businesses
body:
Derek, Democrats have said some pretty radical things, but the latest anti-business comments really take the cake.[](https://click.ml.mailersend.com/link/c/YT0xMjQyNjgyNzYxNTcxOTk3MTAyJmM9bzd4NCZlPTE5MjgmYj0yNzYzMjcwNjYmZD1kNGoyaTVw.RNxOQEc971L7JnNgzoughykrzNVSFZzMuhTWDPqaVcY)Rep. Soucy and any Democrat refusing to dispute his recent comments about New Hampshire’s small business community are out-of-touch with the reality of the Granite State. Before I got involved in politics, I was a small business owner, and I know that fellow small business owners in New Hampshire are making sacrifices everyday to overcome numerous challenges and hurdles. **We should not be demonizing job creators and the backbone of our communities.** Governor Chris Sununu was right to veto a disastrous budget with tax hikes that the Democrat caucus blindly supported without doing their due-diligence. We're going to hold Democrats across New Hampshire who agree with Rep. Soucy accountable in 2020, [but we need the resources to do so.](https://click.ml.mailersend.com/link/c/YT0xMjQyNjgyNzYxNTcxOTk3MTAyJmM9bzd4NCZlPTE5MjgmYj0yNzYzMjcwNjgmZD13MHgwZDl1.7jC4JPg5Zz0vC4q7SPz38XhNeMI796il83pMRsLjoIM) [Can you donate $25 today to help us defend our New Hampshire small businesses?](https://click.ml.mailersend.com/link/c/YT0xMjQyNjgyNzYxNTcxOTk3MTAyJmM9bzd4NCZlPTE5MjgmYj0yNzYzMjcwNzAmZD13NG4zcTZu.vgir4dDiJVQUxdNtW_RO-CEhohkIn8ZNATEHNf7D-X0) Sincerely, Stephen Stepanek
NHGOP Chairman
* * *

###### **New Hampshire Republican State Committee** | 10 Water Street, Concord

[](https://secure.314action.org/page/m/29bb47c8/4bd7760e/42694141/77e16116/4221022269/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/?g=v87XQ8Qot1BdKB-_uknky5A "https://secure.314action.org/page/s/314_EM_EN_190911_NyeMethaneTrump_U1_X5?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=naughton&utm_content=5&utm_campaign=314_EM_EN_190911_NyeMethaneTrump_U1_X5&source=314_EM_EN_190911_NyeMethaneTrump_U1_X5")

**Big news! The 2020 Indiana Republican Party State Convention is set -- and we're returning to Indianapolis on June 20, 2020!**

After going on the road to Evansville in 2018, we'll be returning to Indiana's capital city next year for our biennial convention. And we'll be bringing 1,800 Hoosier Republicans like you from across Indiana with us! At next year's convention, delegates will be voting to nominate our party's candidate for lieutenant governor and attorney general.

**Check out the[Official Call for next year's convention](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e90a42eda3&e=1acd9bb3d6) \-- which includes the number of delegates from each county -- and more details you'll want to know. **

And, like always, we'll also have additional activities for delegates on the Friday night before convention -- June 19 -- as well!

**Interested in being a convention delegate next year? Make sure that you run to represent your county in the May 2020 primary!**

More details about the 2020 Indiana Republican Party State Convention will be released in the coming months, so make sure you keep your eyes on your inbox, and continue to follow _your_ Indiana Republican Party on [Facebook](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e9434d55ed&e=1acd9bb3d6) and [Twitter](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4ac6fd6e54&e=1acd9bb3d6)!

name: Bobby Jindal
subject: We cannot forget, we will not forget
body:
America will never be as it was before the planes hit those towers.
Hello Everyone,
As we approach twenty years since terrorists attacked our nation in an attempt to destroy us, we must again affirm that we will never forget the events of that tragic day.

America will never be as it was before the planes hit those towers. The American people were irrevocably changed that day. In some ways, we are stronger now than we were, but we would all trade that strength to go back and prevent that tragedy.

Life goes on; we all know that. But, please take a moment today to reach out to someone you love. Please take a moment to reflect on what we lost that day, and how far we have come.

God Bless

Bobby Jindal

name: Sri Preston Kulkarni
subject: Never forget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sri for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/wQtrO4KJ-AtY1vr_TK-8s7bPwk6oU8XU7Ec2WBlv1D0=/QYcgHNnMcv6y8ukiEgk6PSqvBAmSEJadkogFWL_2qvc=)
---
Friend, Today we commemorate one of our nation's most tragic anniversaries. Like so many Americans, September 11, 2001 is forever in my memory. I was in college in Austin, and will never forget the feeling of making frantic calls to friends and family in New York that could not go through. And I will never stop being grateful for the relief when I heard their voices again, a relief some families never got. Beyond the immediate shock and grief, we will never forget that day because, in a moment of crisis, our nation came together -- from the neighbors who simply offered a shoulder to cry on, to the first responders who gave their lives running into the chaos -- and together we rose above the tide of violence and hate to show that we, all of us, were better than that and stronger than that. Two years later, I proudly joined the United States Foreign Service. In that role I served tours overseas in Iraq, Israel, Russia, Taiwan, and Jamaica, promoting American values, such as women's rights, a free press, and religious tolerance. Now, I'm answering another call to serve. I'm running for Congress to keep fighting against hate, bringing our nation together, and promoting the values that unite us as a society. On this anniversary we must take the time to mourn, but I hope you'll also join me in remembering the power of public service and love for our neighbors to overcome hate. In solidarity,
Sri Preston Kulkari

subject: President Trump, First Lady Lead 9-11 Memorial Service at White House
body:
"Trump led a moment of silence that began at 8:46 a.m., the moment the first plane, American Airlines Flight 11, struck the North Tower of the..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7939918ac5&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump, First Lady Lead 9-11 Memorial Service at White House**

“President Donald Trump participated in somber memorial services on the 18th anniversary of the 9/11 attacks with first lady Melania Trump at his side,” Debra J. Saunders reports for the _Las Vegas Journal-Review_.

The White House flag flew at half-staff, while families of victims joined the First Couple on the South Lawn, facing the National Mall. “Trump led a moment of silence that began at 8:46 a.m., the moment the first plane, American Airlines Flight 11, struck the North Tower of the World Trade Center in New York City on Sept. 11, 2001.”

After the ceremony, President Trump traveled to the Pentagon to give remarks.

[Click here to see the moving photo.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=69f46734f6&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “By important measurements life is getting better in the United States, especially for those at the bottom of the income ladder,” James Freeman writes in [_The Wall Street Journal_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bb1bd2cab1&e=3ee4985eea). “The great news of the day arrives in a report from the Census Bureau: . . . ‘In 2018, for the first time in 11 years, the official poverty rate was significantly lower than 2007.’”
---
|
---
| “Many Americans may not know it, but our hemisphere is experiencing one of the largest humanitarian crises it has ever seen. The failed Nicolas Maduro regime in Venezuela has ended in the way so many corrupt socialist regimes have: in bankruptcy, lies and suffering,” Health and Human Services Secretary Alex Azar writes for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=149869624b&e=3ee4985eea). “The Trump administration has made it a top priority to aid the people of Venezuela and support governments across the region.”
---
|
---
| “It’s National Historically Black Colleges and Universities [HBCU] Week, and President Trump is marking the occasion like no president in history,” including with yesterday’s speech at the annual HBCU Conference, Paris Dennard writes in [_The Daily Caller_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=752d6e4d4f&e=3ee4985eea). “Unfortunately, President Trump will never get the credit he deserves for all he has done for HBCUs,” from giving HBCU representatives a foot inside the White House to relieving more than $300 million of past hurricane debt for four schools.
---
|
---
| “The third quarter report of the U.S. Chamber of Commerce's Small Business Index shows ‘employers are experiencing high levels of confidence about the state of their local economies and their financial future’ during President Donald Trump's tenure,” Cameron Cawthorne reports for [_The Washington Free Beacon_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b8f37d562a&e=3ee4985eea). The index also suggests that “over two-thirds of U.S. small business owners are optimistic about the local and national business environment, as well as about their own companies' future.”
---
|

| | Many years have passed since the horrific terrorist attacks that took place on September 11, 2001. It is our duty to remember that day and talk to our children about it so that the lives lost and the heroic acts performed will never be forgotten.

I am grateful for the opportunity to live in this great nation and for the many men and women who dedicate their lives to ensuring our safety. May God be with those who still grieve for their loved ones lost and those who still fight to protect our freedoms.

Yours in the fight!

Jake LaTurner

name: Francis Conole
subject: America is worth fighting for
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Conole for Congress
body:
| [](conoleforcongress.com)
---

| | I will never forget 9/11. The sadness, the fear, the anxiety, and the resolve and sense of togetherness that followed.

I had just graduated from the Naval Academy and we knew the world was about to change. I knew the calling I felt to serve was the right path. I would deploy shortly after 9/11 to the Middle East placed in charge of almost 50 sailors. In the aftermath, so many would pay the ultimate sacrifice. I lost friends, classmates, and fellow service members. I remember the firefighters and police officers putting their lives at risk to save the innocent.

I remember amidst the horror, this awful day brought Americans together in a way we had not seen in long time. We have been through dark moments and come through them together. Together, I believe we can pave the way to a brighter future. America is worth fighting for.

Yours in service,

Francis

|
---|---|---

|

name: Dan Baer
subject: That day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
Getting ready for work. Walking to school.
|
---
[ ](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827db/232dfd1/8252dec/62a1c31e/3210142734/VEsH/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://www.danforcolorado.com")

| | Dear Friend, Getting ready for work. Walking to school. Waking up. Anyone old enough remembers where we were. We also remember who we were right before we heard. A parent trying to wrangle kids. A new employee worried about a meeting with the boss. A high school student daydreaming about college. September 11, 2001 was one of those rare days in history where there is a clear before and after, where tragedy punctuated our individual lives and changed the trajectory of our country. The loss of those who perished upended families and cities. The attack on our values precipitated a collective loss of innocence. The children born that day become legal adults today--they have only lived in the "after". Their young lives have seen two major wars, the Great Recession, and the election of Donald Trump. Some of them will serve our country in the military, they may yet be sent to a war that started just weeks after they were born, a war that has grown up with them; for them, war has always been there. In the last two decades we have allowed mistakes made after 9/11, including the invasion of Iraq, to eat away at the social fabric of our country. In addition to the human toll, we have spent far more of our treasure on war and on tax breaks for the wealthy than we have on preparing America and Americans for the rest of the 21st century. Those we lost on September 11 never saw the after. Would they be proud of where we are today? Let us commit to one another to honor their memory by seeking to heal our country, by effecting the change we need, and by delivering a better "after" for those who turn 18 today, and for all Americans. Sincerely, Dan |
---

name: Bill Weld
subject: never forget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Weld 2020 Presidential Campaign Committee · United States
body:
|
---
Team — Today marks the eighteenth anniversary of the terrorist attacks on September 11, 2001, one of the darkest moments in our nation’s history. Grief and loss never entirely fade, and today is a day we keep in our thoughts all those who lost loved ones to that terrible atrocity. We will never forget them. As we remember that terrible day, let us reflect — just for a moment — on the kind of country that our enemies attacked, and the way of life that we, when we've responded, have found worth fighting for. We're a country of laws, where everyone, even those who make and enforce the laws, is bound by the law. We're also a country of free people who make our own way. We take advantage of the simple opportunity to work hard to try to become whatever we want to make ourselves, taking our chances along the way. Since our country was founded, through all the changes, we've been proud to be a nation of strivers, of survivors, of risk-takers, of entrepreneurs, of inventors, of trailblazers. We welcome anybody who wants to live by those laws, take those chances and forge those trails, as our brothers and sisters in freedom. We stand alongside those on other shores who aspire to that same freedom. That freedom creates many differences among us — that's what freedom does. And oftentimes we get it wrong, sometimes for ourselves, sometimes for other people — but we fix what's broken and try to do better, because that's what you do when you have a promise to keep and a standard to live up to. And we take a common pride in our democracy, our free elections, our Constitution, our Bill of Rights, our promise of a better future. We stand against anybody — from outside or within — who seeks to shatter those things. We stood together that way on that day eighteen years ago, and we still can today. And those who want to destroy us must know now, as they learned then: we're coming for them, and we're coming for them together. — Bill

name: Tim Ryan
subject: I remember where I was
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:
|
---
18 years ago today, the core of our country was shaken. Like many of you, I remember where I was on that fateful day. I remember feeling helpless, devastated, and angry that such a cowardly attack would be launched on thousands of innocent American lives.

Today, we remember those lives -- from the men and women who perished in the Twin Towers and at the Pentagon, the courageous first responders who fought to save them, and the heroes on Flight 93. We also pay tribute to the citizens of New York, and Washington D.C., who watched in horror as the sky filled with smoke and fire -- they showed the world that day what strength and resolve in times of utter chaos looks like.

Our country was tested on September 11, 2001. But we did not cower in fear. We did not retreat. We came out stronger and more determined -- and more than anything, we joined together and demonstrated an impenetrable united front. We showed the world what it meant to be American.

I will never forget. We will never forget.

With gratitude,
Tim
|

body:
Leopold served as Chief of Staff for Rep. Lynn Jenkins for 10 years, and as Steve Watkins' campaign manager in 2018 | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/a08491175680/were-running-for-congress-1324145?e=1050d051da)

---
| | **LaTurner for Congress Announces Pat Leopold to Serve as General Consultant for Campaign**
_Leopold served as Chief of Staff for Rep. Lynn Jenkins for 10 years, and as Steve Watkins campaign manager in 2018 _
We are pleased to announce the addition of Pat Leopold to the LaTurner for Congress team, serving as a general consultant for the campaign. Pat served as Chief of Staff for Rep. Lynn Jenkins for 10 years and most recently ran Steve Watkins' campaign for Congress.

Pat has tremendous experience winning elections in the 2nd Congressional district and will be a huge asset to our team. We are delighted he has chosen to join us as we work hard to earn the support of the voters and ensure that the seat stays in Republican hands next November.

Pat has lived and worked in Kansas’ 2nd Congressional district for well over a decade. He managed the campaign of former Rep. Lynn Jenkins in 2008 and served as her chief of staff until her retirement last year. In the fall of 2018, fearing the 2nd District could slip into Democrat hands, Leopold took a leave of absence from Jenkins' office to manage the general election campaign of LaTurner’s primary opponent, Rep. Steve Watkins.

Here's what Pat had to say about joining the team:

“The last thing we can afford is Kansas sending another vote for Nancy Pelosi to Congress. The 2nd District seat is again in jeopardy of a Democrat takeover, and Jake is the best candidate to lock this seat down for Republicans,” said Leopold. “I’ve known Jake for more than 10 years and respect his vision for our state and country. He is a conservative to his core and I trust him to do what is right and stand up for Kansans in Washington D.C. I am proud to be by his side during this campaign."

We have great momentum already in this very important race, and look forward to continuing to build coalitions across Kansas. Thank you for your continued support.

Yours in the fight!
Jake LaTurner

subject: Top five things that mattered this week:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
Hey team,
We've been busy all week preparing for the third debate tomorrow night! Before the Boss takes the stage, wanted you to have the latest updates from the past week:
**The third DNC debate is tomorrow.**
Cory will be on the debate stage with Joe Biden, Elizabeth Warren, Kamala Harris, Pete Buttigieg, Bernie Sanders, Andrew Yang, Beto O'Rourke, Amy Klobuchar, and Julian Castro. Tune in to ABC at 8 p.m. ET to watch the debate live. **Text DEBATE to 40203 to receive live debate updates. Also, be sure to follow along on social media and tweet using hashtags #Cory2020 and #DemDebate during the debate!**
**Take our debate survey.**
This is your last chance to tell Cory about which issues you want to hear about during the debate. If you have any advice for him before he takes the stage, you can let him know by **[filling out this survey](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/Debate-feedback?source=em_x_20190911_AD_20190911-x-AD-ADupdate-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**. It's very important to Cory that he hears directly from supporters like you. **[Complete the survey before it's too late!](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/Debate-feedback?source=em_x_20190911_AD_20190911-x-AD-ADupdate-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**
**Did you see Cory during CNN 's Climate Town Hall?**
Last week, Cory participated in CNN's Climate Town Hall. With a focus on environmental justice, Cory made it clear -- we can only tackle the big issues like climate change if we work together. That's how we'll beat Trump, that's how we'll rise. As Cory said during the town hall, **" Climate is not a separate issue. It is the lens through which we must do everything." [Read and share what _The New York Times_ wrote about Cory's performance.](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0b0e/5fca0d19/749273ff/291e334c/2137521220/VEsF/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://www.nytimes.com/live/2019/democrats-climate-town-hall/cory-booker-climate-change")**
**Cory 's speech at the New Hampshire Democratic Party State Convention.**
This past weekend, Cory spent a lot of time in the critical early voting state of New Hampshire. He gave a powerful speech about how he's the candidate in this race best positioned to bring people together. Cory's remarks brought the enthusiastic crowd to their feet. It's a must-watch, team. It's also worth pointing out that Cory now has 81 endorsements in the state -- leading all 2020 candidates in New Hampshire endorsements. **[Check out his speech from this weekend.](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0b0e/5fca0d19/749273ff/291e334b/2137521220/VEsC/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://www.c-span.org/video/?463579-4/senator-booker-hampshire-democratic-party-convention")**
**We 're getting close to end-of-quarter.**
At the end of this month, we'll be closing the books on our third quarter. This means we'll be closing out an important fundraising period and reporting our financial numbers publicly. It's critical that we have a strong showing so we can keep our momentum going as we finish the quarter. **[Chip in today to help us meet our end-of-quarter goal before the FEC filing deadline.](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-september?refcode=em_x_20190911_AD_20190911-x-AD-ADupdate-actives_actives_l3&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**
Hope you're as excited as we are for debate night. Be sure to tune in tomorrow!
Talk soon,
Addisu
Addisu Demissie
Campaign Manager
Cory 2020

subject: President Trump leads moment of silence for September 11 victims
body:
Eighteen years ago, America and the world were forever changed. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b76e51f894&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • September 11, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump leads moment of silence for September 11 victims**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7a7cde3e97&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **Eighteen years ago,** America and the world were forever changed. Confronted by a series of cowardly acts of terror, the United States once again demonstrated to the world the unmatched strength of our resolve and the indomitable power of our character.

**This morning** , President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump—along with Americans all across our country—honored and mourned the nearly 3,000 lives we lost.

**On the South Lawn** of the White House, the President and First Lady led a moment of silence beginning at 8:46 a.m.—the exact time when the first plane struck the North Tower of the World Trade Center. They were joined by families of victims from that day, looking out onto the National Mall as the White House flag flew at half-staff behind them.

**Shortly after,** the First Couple traveled to Arlington, Virginia, where the President spoke at the September 11th Pentagon Observance Ceremony. Addressing both victims’ families and members of our Armed Services, he explained how this day is seared into the soul of every American who lived through it. For President Trump, the day carries particular significance, as he witnessed the events in New York as a resident of the city.

**“I vividly remember** when I first heard the news,” he said, adding that he was looking out a Midtown Manhattan window when he watched the second plane crash into the World Trade Center. Soon after, like many other New Yorkers, he went down to Ground Zero, looking to help out in any small way he could.

**“But for the families** who join us, this is your anniversary of personal and permanent loss. It's the day that has replayed in your memory a thousand times over,” he said.

**“You waited, you prayed,** you answered that most dreaded call, and your life changed forever. To each of you: The First Lady and I are united with you in grief. We come here in the knowledge that we cannot erase the pain or reverse the evil of that dark and wretched day, but we offer you all that we have: our unwavering loyalty, our undying devotion, and our eternal pledge that your loved ones will never, ever be forgotten.”

**In the midst****of the attack** , the world also witnessed the awesome power of American strength and resolve. It could be seen in the perseverance of the brave first responders in both New York and at the Pentagon, as well as in the 40 passengers and crew aboard Flight 93.

**One such hero** was retired Army Colonel Rick Rescorla, who gave his own life to save many others. After a decorated military career serving in the Vietnam War, Rick went on to become the Vice President for Security at Morgan Stanley in the World Trade Center. On September 11, Rick died while leading people in the South Tower to safety.

**It’s estimated** that his actions that day saved 2,700 lives. This morning, President Trump honored Rick’s incredible sacrifice by awarding him the Presidential Citizens Medal.

**“The future** of our nation is secured through the vigilance of our people: The brave men and women who tore through the gates of hell to save the hurt and the wounded . . . Each of your lives tells the story of courage and character, virtue and valor, resilience and resolve, loyalty and love,” the President said.

**“This morning,** we make a sacred vow to carry on this noble legacy.”

[What the World Learned on September 11, 2001](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dcd4c671c1&e=3ee4985eea)

** President Trump:** [On 9/11, “the world witnessed the power of American defiance”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bfb03d537e&e=3ee4985eea)

| Hello,

We need to ask you a quick favor.

Ed Markey is facing a crucial FEC fundraising deadline this month, and his public report must reflect the widespread grassroots support Ed has to keep fighting for our values.

body:
| | |
---
| Greetings from Houston, team -- I wanted to send a note of gratitude before tomorrow's debate. Truly, from the bottom of my heart, thank you for everything you've done to fund our campaign efforts. **Every single dollar goes directly toward moving this team forward: advertising, payroll, events, travel, and so on. If you 'd like to contribute to [help us hit our fundraising goals before the FEC quarterly deadline, chip in here:](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-september?refcode=em_fr_20190911_CB_20190911-fr-CB-Debate-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-september](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-september?refcode=em_fr_20190911_CB_20190911-fr-CB-Debate-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** The day I decided to run for president, I made it very clear: **this will be a campaign for and by the people.** We won't take money from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or pharma executives. We will spend every dollar wisely. We will treat our campaign staff with dignity -- that means fully-funded health insurance, paid family leave, and affordable coverage for mental health care. We will work with all Americans to ensure their voices are being heard. We won't back down from big fights, and we certainly won't look away from the toughest issues. We'll face them head-on and bring our values to the forefront of the national conversation. I'm proud to say that we've lived up to those promises. That's what this campaign is about, team. It's about bringing people together -- because that's how we'll beat Trump in 2020, and that's how we'll _rise together._ **We 're in this for the long run, so please have my back as I prepare to take the podium tomorrow night. Can you chip in before midnight tonight to help us hit our FEC quarterly goal? Here's a link:** **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-september](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-september?refcode=em_fr_20190911_CB_20190911-fr-CB-Debate-actives_actives_l3&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** Thank you,
Cory [](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-september?refcode=em_fr_20190911_CB_20190911-fr-CB-Debate-actives_actives_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

This campaign is moving quickly. It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. We believe these emails are an important way to stay in touch, but if at any point you'd like to stop getting these updates, you can unsubscribe [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0b08/5fca0d19/7482a670/291e32f6/896691824/VEsP/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/unsubscribe").
Is this not your preferred email address? You can update your contact info [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0b08/5fca0d19/7482a670/291e32ea/896691824/VEsHBQ/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://corybooker.com/join?source=em_emfooter").
